## Executive Summary

The data center cooling market is undergoing rapid transformation, driven by the surge in AI and high-performance computing (HPC) workloads, escalating sustainability mandates, and the need for higher rack densities. Schneider Electric has emerged as a leader in this space, bolstering its position through strategic acquisitions such as Motivair, which significantly expanded its liquid cooling capabilities and product portfolio. The company's investments in advanced cooling technologies—including Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), immersion cooling, and direct-to-chip solutions—have positioned it at the forefront of innovation, particularly for AI-optimized data centers. Schneider’s EcoStruxure platform and DCIM integration enable intelligent, energy-efficient cooling orchestration, supporting both operational efficiency and sustainability goals. The company’s partnership with NVIDIA has yielded reference designs tailored for high-density AI deployments, further accelerating market adoption and reducing energy consumption. Financially, Schneider’s data center segment has shown robust growth, with cooling solutions contributing a rising share of orders and revenue, underpinned by strong margins and cash flow generation. Real-world deployments, such as those at University College Dublin and DataXion, demonstrate tangible benefits including reduced PUE, lower maintenance costs, and improved workforce comfort. Schneider’s commitment to sustainability is evident in its AI-optimized designs, lifecycle intelligence integration, and participation in global decarbonization initiatives. The company’s expansion of manufacturing capacity, global channel reach, and ecosystem partnerships further solidify its ability to deliver comprehensive, scalable cooling solutions. As the market is projected to grow substantially—reaching tens of billions by the mid-2030s—Schneider’s integrated approach, technological leadership, and focus on sustainability position it as a key enabler of next-generation, energy-efficient data centers.

## 1. Executive Overview

This Executive Overview provides a high-level summary of Schneider Electric’s strategic positioning and key developments in the data center cooling market from 2022 to 2025, with a focus on their advanced cooling solutions tailored for AI and HPC environments. It highlights major strategic moves—including the acquisition of Motivair, partnership with NVIDIA, and U.S. expansion—and summarizes recent financial performance and the contribution of the data center segment, particularly the CDU market, to Schneider Electric’s overall growth.

### 1.1. Updated market positioning of Schneider Electric in advanced cooling for AI and HPC environments

Schneider Electric has significantly strengthened its position in advanced cooling for AI and HPC environments between 2022 and 2025. The acquisition of Motivair in late 2024 (75% stake for $850 million) brought deep expertise in liquid cooling, including CDUs, rear-door heat exchangers, cold plates, and HDUs, enabling Schneider to offer end-to-end thermal management from chip to chiller [1](https://www.motivaircorp.com/news/schneider-electric-acquires-controlling-interest-i/).

In September 2025, Schneider unveiled a comprehensive liquid cooling portfolio under the “Motivair by Schneider Electric” brand, featuring megawatt-scale CDUs (105 kW to 2.5 MW), ChilledDoor rear-door heat exchangers, Dynamic Cold Plates, HDUs, chillers, and EcoStruxure software—designed specifically for AI and HPC workloads [2](https://www.motivaircorp.com/news/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio/).

These developments position Schneider Electric as a leading provider of integrated liquid cooling solutions capable of addressing the thermal demands of AI Factories and high-density computing environments, with offerings that are ahead of current market density requirements and aligned with future growth trajectories [2](https://www.motivaircorp.com/news/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio/).

### 1.2. Key strategic highlights: Motivair acquisition, Nvidia partnership, U.S. expansion

Key strategic initiatives from 2022 to 2025 include:

1. Acquisition of Motivair: In October 2024, Schneider Electric acquired a 75% controlling interest in Motivair for $850 million, with plans to acquire the remaining 25% by 2028. This move expanded Schneider’s liquid cooling capabilities and reinforced its data center infrastructure portfolio [1](https://www.motivaircorp.com/news/schneider-electric-acquires-controlling-interest-i/).

2. NVIDIA Partnership: In March 2024, Schneider Electric announced a collaboration with NVIDIA to develop AI data center reference designs optimized for accelerated computing clusters. These designs focus on high-power distribution, liquid cooling systems, and controls to support extreme-density AI workloads, enhancing performance, scalability, and energy efficiency [3](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-collaborates-with-nvidia-on-designs-for-ai-data-centers-65f79837d71f94a78209bfaa).

3. U.S. Manufacturing Expansion: In June 2025, Motivair by Schneider Electric opened its fourth U.S. production facility in Buffalo, New York. This expansion strengthens the U.S. supply chain, increases manufacturing throughput, and accelerates time-to-market for AI and HPC cooling solutions [4](https://www.motivaircorp.com/news/motivair-by-schneider-electric-expands-u-s-manufacturing-footprint/).

### 1.3. Recent financial performance and data center segment contribution

Schneider Electric’s data center segment has been a key growth driver from 2022 through 2025:

- In 2023, the Data Center & Networks end-market accounted for approximately 21% of Schneider’s group orders, equivalent to around €8 billion [5](https://www.reuters.com/markets/deals/schneider-electric-buy-data-centre-cooling-firm-motivair-850-million-2024-10-17/).
- In 2024, data centers represented about 24% of orders, up from 23% in 2023 and 19% in 2022, reflecting accelerating demand [6](https://www.ft.com/content/c0f318d5-7daa-449f-9a53-d37c57029489).
- In Q2 2025, Schneider reported 8.3% organic revenue growth to €10.01 billion, with double-digit sales growth in the data center segment and strong performance in cooling solutions, particularly through Motivair [7](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-confirms-2025-outlook-data-centres-drive-growth-2025-07-31/).

Title: Data Center Segment Order Share (2022–2024)

| Year | Data Center Order Share (%) | Approximate Order Value (€ billion) |
|:----:|:---------------------------:|-------------------------------:|
| 2022 | 19.0 | N/A |
| 2023 | 23.0 | 8.0 |
| 2024 | 24.0 | N/A |

These figures underscore the growing importance of the data center cooling segment—especially CDUs—in Schneider Electric’s revenue mix, reinforcing its strategic focus and investment in this high-growth area.

## 2. Schneider Electric SE Company Profile

This section provides a detailed examination of Schneider Electric’s corporate strategy in the AI-era infrastructure and liquid cooling domain, its global footprint and manufacturing expansion, updated financial indicators with emphasis on data center revenue share, and its leadership and governance in digital infrastructure transformation. All information reflects the most recent data available as of November 19, 2025.

### 2.1. Corporate strategy in AI-era infrastructure and liquid cooling

Schneider Electric has strategically positioned itself at the forefront of AI-era infrastructure by integrating advanced liquid cooling technologies into its data center offerings. Following its acquisition of a majority stake in Motivair in early 2025, the company unveiled a comprehensive liquid cooling portfolio under the “Motivair by Schneider Electric” brand, encompassing Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), rear-door heat exchangers, dynamic cold plates, chillers, and AI-ready software and services [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). The CDU range spans from 105 kW to 2.5 MW, enabling support for the most demanding AI and HPC workloads and already deployed in six of the world’s top ten supercomputers [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Schneider Electric has also co-developed AI infrastructure reference designs with NVIDIA, integrating power management and liquid cooling controls compatible with NVIDIA Mission Control and GB300 NVL72 systems. These designs support rack densities up to 142 kW and facilitate seamless OT/IT interoperability, accelerating deployment timelines and enhancing operational efficiency [9](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/18/3152299/0/en/index.html). Additionally, earlier reference designs for GB200 NVL72 clusters support up to 132 kW per rack and emphasize energy efficiency and sustainability [10](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-announces-new-solutions-to-address-the-energy-and-sustainability-challenges-spurred-by-ai-674f1aaeda55de201f0ca8c4).

This strategy underscores Schneider Electric’s commitment to delivering end-to-end, AI-optimized cooling infrastructure—from chip to chiller—backed by software platforms like EcoStruxure for intelligent thermal management and lifecycle optimization [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

### 2.2. Global footprint and manufacturing expansion (e.g., Buffalo, U.S.)

Schneider Electric has expanded its global manufacturing footprint to support growing demand for liquid cooling solutions. Motivair, now under Schneider Electric, opened its fourth production facility in Buffalo, New York, in 2025, while simultaneously scaling manufacturing capacity in Italy and India—tripling output and reducing lead times globally [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

The company’s global supply chain and technical validation infrastructure ensure rigorous testing of liquid cooling units under real-world thermal loads, with each unit flushed and performance-validated before deployment [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). Schneider Electric also supports pilot deployments such as the in-rack direct-to-chip liquid cooling CDU system at Equinix’s HK1 data center in Hong Kong, delivering up to 150 kW per rack and achieving PUEs of 1.2 or lower—demonstrating its global reach and technical leadership [11](https://www.equinix.com/newsroom/press-releases/hongkong/2025/09/equinix-dell-technologies-and-schneider-electric-support-ai-infrastructure-in-hong-kong).

### 2.3. Updated financial indicators and data center revenue share

Schneider Electric’s financial performance through 2025 reflects robust growth driven by its data center segment. In Q2 2025, the company reported 8.3 % organic revenue growth to €10.01 billion, with double-digit sales growth in the data center segment, particularly in cooling solutions via Motivair [7](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-confirms-2025-outlook-data-centres-drive-growth-2025-07-31/). For full-year 2024, total revenue reached €38.15 billion, with energy management up 11.5 % organically to €31.13 billion; the data center segment was highlighted as a key growth driver [12](https://www.wsj.com/business/earnings/schneider-electric-profit-revenue-beat-market-views-233317b1).

According to Dell’Oro Group, Schneider Electric and Vertiv are virtually tied for global market share in data center physical infrastructure, with liquid cooling revenue more than doubling in Q1 2025 and the total liquid cooling market projected to exceed $2.5 billion in 2025 [13](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-physical-infrastructure-grew-17-percent-yy-in-1q-2025-driven-by-ai-buildout-according-to-delloro-group-302497877.html).

Table: Schneider Electric Financial and Market Metrics

Title: Schneider Electric Data Center Segment Financial Metrics

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|:---|---:|:---|
| Q2 2025 Organic Revenue Growth | 8.3 % | Total revenue €10.01 billion [7](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-confirms-2025-outlook-data-centres-drive-growth-2025-07-31/) |
| 2024 Total Revenue | €38.15 billion | Energy management €31.13 billion [12](https://www.wsj.com/business/earnings/schneider-electric-profit-revenue-beat-market-views-233317b1) |
| Liquid Cooling Market Size (2025) | >$2.5 billion | Global market projection [13](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-physical-infrastructure-grew-17-percent-yy-in-1q-2025-driven-by-ai-buildout-according-to-delloro-group-302497877.html) |
| Market Share Position | Tied with Vertiv | Data center physical infrastructure [13](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-physical-infrastructure-grew-17-percent-yy-in-1q-2025-driven-by-ai-buildout-according-to-delloro-group-302497877.html) |

This data underscores the growing importance of the data center cooling segment within Schneider Electric’s overall business and its competitive positioning in a rapidly expanding market.

### 2.4. Leadership and governance in digital infrastructure transformation

Schneider Electric demonstrates leadership in digital infrastructure transformation through strategic governance and innovation. The company’s integration of Motivair and collaboration with NVIDIA reflect a governance model that prioritizes co-engineering, interoperability, and sustainability in AI infrastructure [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Its EcoStruxure platform and reference design frameworks embody a governance approach that aligns operational technology (OT) and information technology (IT), enabling digital twins, real-time control, and lifecycle optimization across power and cooling systems [9](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/18/3152299/0/en/index.html). Schneider Electric’s emphasis on rigorous testing, global manufacturing standards, and sustainability consulting further reinforces its governance in delivering resilient, efficient, and sustainable digital infrastructure [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Through these initiatives, Schneider Electric positions itself not only as a technology provider but as a trusted partner guiding the transformation of data center infrastructure in the AI era.

## 3. Data Center Cooling Market Analysis

This section provides a comprehensive analysis of the global data center cooling market between 2022 and 2025, focusing on market size, growth forecasts, key drivers, regulatory and environmental pressures, and emerging technology trends relevant to Schneider Electric’s CDU offerings.

### 3.1. Market size, CAGR, and forecasts (2025–2032/2035)

The global data center cooling market has demonstrated robust growth through 2024 and is projected to expand significantly through the early 2030s. Key data points include:

![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

These projections indicate a consistent compound annual growth rate ranging from approximately 11.9% to 17.0%, depending on the forecast horizon and methodology. The market is expected to reach between USD 42 billion and USD 100 billion by the mid-2030s, underscoring strong long-term expansion potential.

Schneider Electric’s CDU offerings—spanning in‑row, in‑rack, floor‑mounted, and other variants—are well-positioned to capture a growing share of this expanding market, particularly as demand shifts toward liquid cooling and modular, high-efficiency solutions.

### 3.2. Drivers: AI workloads, rack power density, sustainability mandates

Several key drivers are fueling the rapid expansion of the data center cooling market between 2022 and 2025:

- AI workloads and high-performance computing: The surge in AI, cloud, and HPC workloads is increasing thermal loads and rack power densities, necessitating advanced cooling solutions. Reports highlight AI, cloud, and IoT as primary growth catalysts [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

- Rising rack power density: Medium-density racks (5–9 kW) currently dominate, but high-density racks (>9 kW) are growing rapidly, especially in hyperscale and AI environments [17](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/06/10/3096351/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Research-Report-2025-Market-to-Surpass-100-Billion-by-2035-Increasing-Number-of-Distributed-or-Edge-Data-Centers-Fueling-Opportunities.html).

- Sustainability mandates and energy efficiency: Increasing regulatory and corporate pressure to reduce energy consumption and carbon emissions is driving adoption of liquid cooling, free cooling, and modular systems [14](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/press-release/data-centers-cooling-market-9519).

These drivers align directly with Schneider Electric’s strategic focus on liquid cooling and CDU technologies, particularly through its Motivair acquisition and AI-optimized reference designs with NVIDIA, which target high-density, energy-efficient deployments.

### 3.3. Regulatory and environmental pressures shaping cooling adoption

Regulatory and environmental pressures are significantly influencing the adoption of efficient cooling solutions:

- Energy efficiency regulations: In regions such as Europe, stringent energy and carbon regulations are accelerating the shift toward green cooling technologies [19](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/industry-reports/data-center-cooling-market-101959).

- Sustainability and water usage concerns: Data centers are under pressure to reduce water usage and improve PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness), prompting adoption of liquid cooling and thermal energy recovery systems [17](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/06/10/3096351/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Research-Report-2025-Market-to-Surpass-100-Billion-by-2035-Increasing-Number-of-Distributed-or-Edge-Data-Centers-Fueling-Opportunities.html).

- Grid and energy constraints: The IEA projects data center electricity demand will rise by ~800 TWh by 2035, straining grids and reinforcing the need for efficient cooling strategies [20](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/forget-ai-keeping-cool-is-bigger-power-sector-problem-2025-06-23/).

Schneider Electric’s compliance with energy efficiency standards and its EcoStruxure platform’s monitoring capabilities support regulatory alignment and environmental performance, reinforcing its competitive positioning in the cooling market.

### 3.4. Technology trends: liquid cooling, immersion, two‑phase, AI‑driven control systems

Technological innovation is reshaping the data center cooling landscape:

- Liquid cooling and immersion: Adoption of liquid and immersion cooling is accelerating, offering superior thermal performance over traditional air-based systems. Immersion cooling is highlighted as a leading solution in future growth [17](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/06/10/3096351/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Research-Report-2025-Market-to-Surpass-100-Billion-by-2035-Increasing-Number-of-Distributed-or-Edge-Data-Centers-Fueling-Opportunities.html).

- Two‑phase and advanced cold plates: Innovations such as single-phase cold plates capable of handling ~4 kW and fanless direct liquid cooling systems reducing cooling power by ~37% are emerging [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

- AI‑driven control systems: Smart cooling systems leveraging AI and IoT for real-time optimization are gaining traction, enabling adaptive control and energy savings [21](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-cooling-market-to-grow-at-a-cagr-of-16-21-from-2025-to-2032--skyquest-technology-consulting-302520514.html).

- Microfluidic cooling: Microsoft’s microfluidic channel technology, reducing peak chip temperatures by up to 65% and outperforming conventional cold plates by up to 3×, represents a cutting-edge advancement in chip-level thermal management [22](https://www.tomshardware.com/pc-components/liquid-cooling/microsoft-develops-breakthrough-chip-cooling-method-microfluidic-channels-can-cut-peak-temps-by-up-to-65-percent-outperform-conventional-cold-plates-by-up-to-3x).

These trends underscore the importance of modular, high-efficiency CDU solutions. Schneider Electric’s Motivair-powered liquid cooling portfolio, combined with AI-optimized control via EcoStruxure, positions the company to capitalize on these technological shifts and meet evolving customer demands.

## 4. Schneider Electric's Cooling Solutions Portfolio

This section analyzes Schneider Electric’s diverse cooling solutions portfolio, focusing on its end‑to‑end liquid cooling ecosystem powered by the acquisition of Motivair, and detailing its range of hardware and software offerings that support megawatt‑scale data center cooling needs.

### 4.1. Overview of end‑to‑end liquid cooling ecosystem (Motivair‑powered)

Schneider Electric’s liquid cooling ecosystem is now fully integrated under the “Motivair by Schneider Electric” brand following its acquisition of a 75 % stake in Motivair in February 2025 for approximately USD 850 million, with plans to acquire the remaining 25 % by 2028 [5](https://www.reuters.com/markets/deals/schneider-electric-buy-data-centre-cooling-firm-motivair-850-million-2024-10-17/). This acquisition brings over 15 years of Motivair’s exascale and accelerated computing cooling expertise into Schneider’s global infrastructure [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

The ecosystem spans from chip‑level cooling to facility‑level infrastructure, offering a unified portfolio that includes Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), rear‑door heat exchangers, dynamic cold plates, chillers, closed‑loop Technology Cooling System (TCS) loops, and liquid‑to‑air Heat Dissipation Units (HDUs), all supported by Schneider’s global supply chain, services, and software platforms [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). This end‑to‑end approach simplifies deployment, reduces operational complexity, and accelerates time‑to‑market for high‑density AI and HPC data center environments [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

### 4.2. Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs): megawatt‑scale capabilities

Schneider Electric’s Motivair‑powered CDUs deliver industry‑leading capacity, scaling from 105 kW up to 2.5 MW per unit, positioning the company ahead of current and near‑term market demand [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). These CDUs are engineered in collaboration with leading silicon and GPU manufacturers, ensuring seamless integration with next‑generation processors and accelerators [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Notably, Motivair’s CDU technology is deployed in six of the world’s top ten supercomputers, underscoring its reliability and performance at scale [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Title: CDU Capacity Range

| Specification | Value |
|:---|---:|
| Minimum CDU capacity | 105 kW |
| Maximum CDU capacity | 2 500 kW (2.5 MW) |

Source: Schneider Electric press releases [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

### 4.3. Rear‑door heat exchangers and dynamic cold plates

Schneider Electric’s ChilledDoor® rear‑door heat exchangers are designed to cool rack densities up to 75 kW, offering a rack‑agnostic solution ideal for GPU‑intensive HPC environments [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). These units enhance cooling efficiency by capturing heat at the rack boundary, reducing reliance on room‑level cooling.

Dynamic® Cold Plates provide precise thermal control at the chip level, enabling efficient heat removal directly from high‑density compute components. These cold plates are part of the integrated liquid cooling ecosystem and support the thermal demands of AI and HPC workloads [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

### 4.4. Liquid‑to‑air HDUs, chillers, and closed‑loop TCS systems

The Liquid‑to‑Air Heat Dissipation Unit (HDU™) is engineered for environments where water access is limited, such as labs or colocation facilities. It delivers 100 kW of heat rejection within a compact 600 mm footprint and can create a water loop to support up to 132 kW of cooling capacity, matching NVIDIA’s NVL144 architecture [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Schneider Electric’s closed‑loop chillers and Technology Cooling System (TCS) loops offer water‑efficient cooling, reducing water usage by millions of gallons per megawatt of cooling demand while delivering up to 20 % higher performance compared to competing solutions [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

### 4.5. Software (EcoStruxure, AI‑powered optimization) and services

Schneider Electric’s EcoStruxure software platform underpins its liquid cooling ecosystem, offering AI‑powered optimization for thermal management, performance, and energy efficiency in mission‑critical data center environments [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Complementing the hardware and software, Schneider Electric and Motivair provide extensive services, including deployment, maintenance, and field support. Motivair brings over a decade of experience in high‑density liquid cooling deployments and maintains a global network of trained technicians and EcoXpert partners—over 600 cooling field service technicians worldwide [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

## 5. Competitive Landscape Analysis

This section analyzes the competitive environment Schneider Electric operates within, focusing on key players, market share dynamics, and product innovation benchmarks.

### 5.1. Key competitors: Vertiv, Asetek, Delta, immersion specialists, etc.

The primary competitors in the data center cooling market alongside Schneider Electric include:

- Vertiv: Historically the global leader in data center cooling, with a 23.5 % market share based on 2018–2019 data, significantly ahead of its nearest rival [23](https://www.vertiv.com/en-emea/about/news-and-insights/news-releases/2020/vertiv-ranked-as-global-leader-in-rapidly-evolving-data-centre-cooling-market/). Vertiv continues to expand its liquid cooling services portfolio to support AI and high-density computing [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

- Asetek: A specialist in liquid cooling, particularly warm-water systems. The liquid cooling market is projected to grow from USD 4.18 billion in 2024 to USD 13.22 billion by 2029 (CAGR 26.5 %) [24](https://www.ainvest.com/news/asetek-strategic-move-liquid-cooling-high-performance-computing-assessing-long-term-growth-potential-2510/). Asetek’s Ingrid platform, launched in May 2025, offers smart thermal sensing and customizable configurations tailored for HPC and AI workloads [24](https://www.ainvest.com/news/asetek-strategic-move-liquid-cooling-high-performance-computing-assessing-long-term-growth-potential-2510/).

- Delta Electronics: Recognized as a key vendor in the global data center cooling market alongside Schneider Electric, Vertiv, and others [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

- Immersion cooling specialists (e.g., Submer, Asperitas, Midas Immersion Cooling): These firms are gaining traction with advanced immersion technologies. Submer invested over USD 6.55 billion in October 2024 to expand its sustainable immersion cooling solutions [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html). Asperitas introduced a direct forced convection immersion system in June 2024 delivering over 3.6 kW per U [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

Schneider Electric competes across these segments through its Motivair-branded liquid cooling portfolio, offering in-row, in-rack, and floor-mounted CDUs, positioning itself as a comprehensive provider in both traditional and advanced cooling technologies.

### 5.2. Comparative market share and growth trajectories

Available data indicates that Vertiv and Schneider Electric are the two leading players in the global data center cooling market, with estimated revenue shares of approximately 22 % and 13 %, respectively [25](https://www.einpresswire.com/article/644344919/data-center-cooling-market-to-surpass-at-a-revenue-of-us-26-07-billion-by-2031). Together, the top four firms hold close to 45 % of the market, indicating a moderately concentrated competitive landscape [25](https://www.einpresswire.com/article/644344919/data-center-cooling-market-to-surpass-at-a-revenue-of-us-26-07-billion-by-2031).

Growth trajectories are shaped by the rapid expansion of the data center cooling market. The global market was valued at USD 16.32 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 40.72 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 16.46 % [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html). Another forecast estimates growth from USD 11.08 billion in 2025 to USD 24.19 billion by 2032 (CAGR 11.8 %) [26](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/10/13/3165440/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Forecast-Report-to-2032-Leading-Companies-Paving-the-Path-for-Innovation-in-Data-Center-Cooling-Solutions.html).

These figures suggest that Schneider Electric, with its ~13 % share, is well-positioned to benefit from strong market expansion, particularly in liquid cooling segments where its Motivair acquisition enhances its growth potential.

### 5.3. Benchmarking of product capabilities and innovation

Schneider Electric’s product capabilities, particularly through its Motivair acquisition, span a full liquid cooling ecosystem including in-row, in-rack, and floor-mounted CDUs, as well as rear-door heat exchangers and dynamic cold plates (context from earlier sections). 

In comparison:

- Vertiv offers a broad thermal management portfolio and has recently expanded its liquid cooling services to support AI and high-density computing [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

- Asetek focuses on warm-water liquid cooling with energy-saving benefits (up to 40 % operational cost reduction) and smart sensing capabilities in its Ingrid platform [24](https://www.ainvest.com/news/asetek-strategic-move-liquid-cooling-high-performance-computing-assessing-long-term-growth-potential-2510/).

- Immersion specialists like Submer and Asperitas lead in immersion cooling innovation, with high-density solutions (e.g., >3.6 kW per U) and large-scale investments [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

Schneider Electric’s strength lies in its integrated ecosystem and ability to serve diverse cooling needs—from traditional CDUs to advanced liquid cooling—while competitors may specialize in narrower segments.

### 5.4. Pricing and value proposition in AI‑driven cooling market

While specific pricing data is not publicly available, the value proposition of Schneider Electric in the AI-driven cooling market can be inferred:

- Schneider Electric offers end-to-end liquid cooling solutions via Motivair, enabling operational efficiencies and scalability for AI workloads.

- Asetek’s warm-water systems reduce energy costs by up to 40 %, offering a compelling value proposition for hyperscale AI deployments [24](https://www.ainvest.com/news/asetek-strategic-move-liquid-cooling-high-performance-computing-assessing-long-term-growth-potential-2510/).

- Immersion cooling providers deliver high-density cooling with potential savings in space and energy, though often at higher upfront investment.

- Vertiv’s broad portfolio and service capabilities may offer competitive pricing through bundled solutions and global support.

Overall, Schneider Electric’s value proposition is anchored in its comprehensive product range, global reach, and integration capabilities, which may offer cost efficiencies and operational flexibility compared to more specialized competitors.

## 6. Financial Analysis for Investors

This section delivers a focused financial overview tailored for investors, emphasizing Schneider Electric’s revenue streams, profitability, capital and R&D investments, and return dynamics specific to its cooling and data center infrastructure business, particularly CDUs.

### 6.1. Revenue breakdown from cooling and data center infrastructure

Schneider Electric’s data center and cooling-related offerings are embedded within its Energy Management division, which delivered strong performance in recent years. In fiscal year 2024, the Group achieved total revenues of €38.15 billion, up 6.3 % reported and 8.4 % organically from €35.90 billion in 2023 [27](https://finanzwire.com/press-release/full-year-2024-results-press-release-3LLakvRKH72). The Energy Management segment, which includes data center infrastructure and cooling solutions, grew organically by approximately 12 %, contributing around €31.13 billion in revenues [27](https://finanzwire.com/press-release/full-year-2024-results-press-release-3LLakvRKH72). 

While Schneider does not publicly disclose standalone revenue figures for CDUs or cooling solutions, data center-related orders accounted for approximately 24 % of total Group orders in 2024, up from 23 % in 2023 and 19 % in 2022 [6](https://www.ft.com/content/c0f318d5-7daa-449f-9a53-d37c57029489). This indicates a rising share of revenue attributable to data center infrastructure, including cooling systems, over the 2022–2025 period.

### 6.2. Profitability and margin trends in cooling segment

Schneider Electric’s overall adjusted EBITA margin improved from 17.9 % in 2023 to 18.6 % in 2024, a 70 bps increase reported and 90 bps organically [27](https://finanzwire.com/press-release/full-year-2024-results-press-release-3LLakvRKH72). The Energy Management division, which houses cooling and data center infrastructure, delivered an adjusted EBITA margin of approximately 22.1 % in 2024, up from 21.6 % in 2023 [27](https://finanzwire.com/press-release/full-year-2024-results-press-release-3LLakvRKH72). 

Although Schneider does not isolate margins for the cooling segment or CDUs specifically, the strong performance of the Energy Management division suggests that cooling solutions, including CDUs, are contributing positively to margin expansion. However, in early 2025, Schneider revised its full-year adjusted EBITA margin outlook downward to 18.7 %–19.0 % due to market volatility and currency headwinds [28](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-cuts-margin-outlook-market-volatility-2025-04-28/).

### 6.3. Capital expenditure and R&D investment in liquid cooling

In 2024, Schneider Electric’s net capital expenditure amounted to €1.364 billion, representing approximately 3.6 % of revenues (2.4 % tangible capex, 1.2 % intangible capex, largely capitalized R&D) [29](https://www.emr-online.com/schneider-electric-schneider-electric-full-year-2024-results/). R&D cash costs totaled €2.260 billion, or about 5.9 % of revenues [29](https://www.emr-online.com/schneider-electric-schneider-electric-full-year-2024-results/). 

While the company does not break out R&D or capex specifically for liquid cooling or CDUs, the acquisition of Motivair in late 2024 for $850 million underscores a strategic investment in liquid cooling technologies [6](https://www.ft.com/content/c0f318d5-7daa-449f-9a53-d37c57029489). Additionally, Schneider’s planned U.S. investment of over $700 million through 2027 includes facility expansions and labs supporting AI data center markets, which likely encompass cooling infrastructure development [30](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-invest-over-700-million-us-power-ai-boom-2025-03-25/).

### 6.4. ROI and payback analysis for cooling solutions

Schneider Electric does not publicly disclose ROI or payback period metrics specific to its cooling solutions or CDUs. However, the broader financial performance provides context: the Group’s free cash flow in 2024 was €4.216 billion, with a cash conversion ratio of 99 % (free cash flow as a percentage of net income) [29](https://www.emr-online.com/schneider-electric-schneider-electric-full-year-2024-results/). 

Given the strong free cash flow generation and margin expansion in the Energy Management division, investments in cooling infrastructure—including CDUs—are likely contributing to attractive returns. Nonetheless, without explicit ROI or payback data for cooling solutions, investors should consider seeking detailed project-level or customer case study data to assess payback timelines and return profiles more precisely.

## 7. Growth Strategy and Innovation

This section examines Schneider Electric’s strategic focus on research and innovation in data center cooling, particularly liquid cooling technologies, and outlines their roadmap for future growth and sustainability.

### 7.1. R&D focus: two‑phase cooling, immersion, AI control systems

Schneider Electric’s R&D strategy emphasizes advanced cooling technologies tailored for AI and high‑density computing environments. In 2025, the company unveiled its comprehensive liquid cooling portfolio under the Motivair by Schneider Electric brand, featuring megawatt‑class Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), rear‑door heat exchangers, dynamic cold plates, chillers, and closed‑loop systems designed for AI workloads exceeding 100 kW per rack and scaling up to 2.5 MW [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). The company is also advancing immersion cooling capabilities through its Eliwell Modicon M172 controllers, enabling efficient management of immersion systems that can reduce server power consumption by 10–20 % and overall power usage by up to 50 % [31](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/05/15/how-to-build-sustainable-data-centers-with-eliwell/). On the AI control front, Schneider Electric collaborates with NVIDIA to co‑develop AI‑optimized reference designs supporting liquid cooling and energy‑efficient architectures, achieving up to 20 % reduction in cooling energy use and 30 % faster deployment timelines [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7).

### 7.2. Patent and IP developments in liquid cooling technologies

While specific patent filings by Schneider Electric in 2025 are not publicly detailed, the company’s acquisition of Motivair and its integration into the liquid cooling portfolio suggest a strategic consolidation of IP in high‑density cooling systems. Motivair’s technology, already deployed in six of the world’s top ten supercomputers and certified for NVIDIA hardware, reflects proprietary innovations in CDU design and integration [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). Additionally, Schneider Electric’s EcoStruxure platform continues to evolve with automated sustainability reporting and digital twin capabilities, reinforcing its IP in software‑enabled cooling optimization [33](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/schneider-electric-adds-automated-sustainability-reporting-to-ecostruxure-it-dcim-software/).

### 7.3. Innovation roadmap and upcoming product launches

Schneider Electric’s innovation roadmap includes several key milestones: the September 2025 launch of the Motivair‑powered liquid cooling portfolio, offering CDUs from 105 kW to 2.5 MW, dynamic cold plates, HDUs, and chillers [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). Earlier in June 2025, the company introduced new EcoStruxure Pod Data Center and Rack Solutions, featuring prefabricated modular architectures, in‑rack liquid cooling, and support for NVIDIA MGX systems [34](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-launches-new-data-center-solutions-to-meet-challenges-of-high-density-ai-and-accelerated-compute-applications-68498becf5a1495e590e4afa). In September 2025, Schneider Electric hosted an Innovation Day in Sydney to showcase next‑generation cooling technologies and AI‑driven infrastructure solutions for the Asia‑Pacific region [35](https://www.se.com/au/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric%E2%80%99s-innovation-day-reinventing-cooling-in-the-ai-era-68c20d0b7ad20ba4980aa241). These developments signal a clear trajectory toward scalable, AI‑ready cooling systems and modular deployment models.

### 7.4. Sustainability and energy‑efficiency initiatives

Sustainability is central to Schneider Electric’s innovation strategy. Their liquid cooling solutions, including chillers and TCS loops, deliver up to 20 % better performance and save millions of gallons of water per megawatt of cooling [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). The company’s partnership with NVIDIA produced reference designs that reduce cooling energy consumption by 20 % and accelerate deployment by 30 % [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7). EcoStruxure IT DCIM now includes automated sustainability reporting to help operators comply with the European Energy Efficiency Directive, enabling tracking of PUE, energy use, and emissions [33](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/schneider-electric-adds-automated-sustainability-reporting-to-ecostruxure-it-dcim-software/). Schneider Electric’s immersion cooling controllers also contribute to energy efficiency, offering significant reductions in power usage and infrastructure costs [31](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/05/15/how-to-build-sustainable-data-centers-with-eliwell/).

## 8. Strategic Partnerships and Acquisitions

This section examines Schneider Electric’s strategic collaborations and acquisitions that reinforce its positioning in the data center cooling industry, particularly in the context of CDUs and AI‑driven infrastructure.

### 8.1. Motivair acquisition and integration strategy

Schneider Electric acquired a 75 % controlling interest in Motivair in October 2024 for USD 850 million, with plans to acquire the remaining 25 % by 2028 [5](https://www.reuters.com/markets/deals/schneider-electric-buy-data-centre-cooling-firm-motivair-850-million-2024-10-17/). Motivair, based in Buffalo, NY, brings deep expertise in liquid cooling technologies—including CDUs, rear‑door heat exchangers, cold plates, and HDUs—used in high‑performance computing and AI environments [5](https://www.reuters.com/markets/deals/schneider-electric-buy-data-centre-cooling-firm-motivair-850-million-2024-10-17/).

Following the acquisition, Schneider Electric integrated Motivair into its Energy Management division and launched the “Motivair by Schneider Electric” brand. In June 2025, Motivair opened its fourth U.S. production facility in Buffalo, strengthening domestic manufacturing and supply chain resilience [4](https://www.motivaircorp.com/news/motivair-by-schneider-electric-expands-u-s-manufacturing-footprint/). In September 2025, Schneider unveiled a comprehensive liquid cooling portfolio—including CDUs scaling from 105 kW to 2.5 MW, rear‑door heat exchangers, HDUs, dynamic cold plates, chillers, and TCS loops—leveraging Motivair’s technology and Schneider’s global reach [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

Strategic benefits include:

- Expanded product breadth across CDU variants and cooling systems, enabling end‑to‑end solutions from chip to chiller.
- Enhanced manufacturing capacity and reduced lead times via U.S. facilities.
- Accelerated time‑to‑market and ROI compression for customers through integrated offerings and global supply chain.
- Strengthened positioning in AI and HPC cooling markets, with Motivair technology already deployed in six of the world’s top ten supercomputers [36](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/schneider-electric-and-motivair-unveil-first-joint-liquid-cooling-portfolio/).

### 8.2. Collaborations: Nvidia reference designs, Microsoft, grid integration

Schneider Electric has established a strategic collaboration with NVIDIA to co‑develop AI‑optimized data center reference designs. Initially announced in March 2024, these designs focus on high‑density AI clusters, integrating high‑power distribution, liquid‑cooling systems, and controls to simplify commissioning and enhance operational reliability [3](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-collaborates-with-nvidia-on-designs-for-ai-data-centers-65f79837d71f94a78209bfaa).

In June 2025, the partnership deepened with R&D initiatives to co‑develop power, cooling, control systems, and high‑density rack solutions aligned with Europe’s AI infrastructure ambitions under the EU’s InvestAI initiative [37](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-accelerates-the-development-and-deployment-of-ai-factories-at-scale-with-nvidia-684989e2ba4801b85e069b2b). Schneider Electric also launched NVIDIA‑enabled rack solutions and reference designs that include Motivair’s liquid cooling portfolio, positioning Schneider as an approved CDU vendor for NVIDIA [37](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-accelerates-the-development-and-deployment-of-ai-factories-at-scale-with-nvidia-684989e2ba4801b85e069b2b).

Independent reporting indicates these reference designs support racks up to 132 kW, reduce cooling energy use by approximately 20 %, and shorten project development time by around 30 % [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7).

While no direct collaboration with Microsoft on cooling was identified, Schneider’s broader infrastructure capabilities—including grid integration and AI‑ready systems—align with large‑scale AI deployments. For example, Schneider is supplying power and cooling systems to AVAIO Digital’s AI‑ready data center campuses in the U.S., supporting rapid deployment and sustainable operations [38](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/avaio-digital-and-schneider-electric-partner-to-build-new-ai-optimized-data-centers-in-u-s-68af2fb2841bc2b2b80556b2).

### 8.3. M&A and alliance activity in cooling and AI infrastructure

Beyond the Motivair acquisition, Schneider Electric has not announced additional cooling‑specific M&A activity through November 2025. The Motivair deal remains the centerpiece of its cooling infrastructure expansion [5](https://www.reuters.com/markets/deals/schneider-electric-buy-data-centre-cooling-firm-motivair-850-million-2024-10-17/).

However, Schneider’s broader strategic investments support AI infrastructure growth. In March 2025, the company committed over USD 700 million to U.S. operations through 2027—expanding manufacturing, energy infrastructure, and job creation across multiple states—to support AI demand and energy security [30](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-invest-over-700-million-us-power-ai-boom-2025-03-25/).

In comparison, competitors like Vertiv have pursued AI‑focused acquisitions—such as acquiring Waylay NV to integrate AI‑powered software for power and cooling management—highlighting the competitive landscape in AI infrastructure consolidation [39](https://www.barrons.com/articles/vertiv-stock-wayland-acquisition-ai-data-center-9ed88998).

### 8.4. Partner ecosystem and channel expansion

Schneider Electric is expanding its partner ecosystem to support growth in cooling solutions through several initiatives:

- The “Motivair by Schneider Electric” brand leverages Schneider’s global digital energy management portfolio, including EcoStruxure software and services, to deliver integrated cooling solutions worldwide [4](https://www.motivaircorp.com/news/motivair-by-schneider-electric-expands-u-s-manufacturing-footprint/).
- The company is training over 600 cooling field service technicians and EcoXpert cooling partners globally to support deployment and maintenance of liquid cooling systems [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).
- Manufacturing expansion in the U.S., Italy, and India triples output capacity and reduces lead times, enabling better channel responsiveness [40](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Collaboration with AVAIO Digital for AI‑ready data center campuses demonstrates channel expansion into hyperscale and colocation markets, with Schneider supplying integrated power and cooling infrastructure [38](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/avaio-digital-and-schneider-electric-partner-to-build-new-ai-optimized-data-centers-in-u-s-68af2fb2841bc2b2b80556b2).

These efforts strengthen Schneider’s ability to deliver turnkey cooling solutions through a robust global partner network, enhancing market reach and customer support.

## 9. Geographic Market Analysis

This section analyzes Schneider Electric’s geographic reach and regional market dynamics for its data center cooling solutions, particularly Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), across key global regions and emerging opportunities.

### 9.1. Regional strengths: North America, Europe, APAC

Schneider Electric demonstrates pronounced regional strengths in the data center cooling market across North America, Europe, and Asia‑Pacific (APAC).

In North America, Schneider benefits from its acquisition of Motivair and substantial U.S. investments exceeding USD 700 million through 2027, reinforcing its manufacturing and supply chain footprint to meet surging AI‑driven cooling demand. North America accounted for approximately 53 % of global data center cooling investment in 2024, with the U.S. alone representing about 98 % of that regional share. Schneider’s strong local production—83 % of goods sold in North America are manufactured locally—further enhances its competitive positioning[30](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-invest-over-700-million-us-power-ai-boom-2025-03-25/).

In Europe, Schneider leverages its EcoStruxure platform and sustainability credentials to address stringent regulatory and energy‑efficiency mandates. Germany led the European data center cooling market in 2024 with around 20 % share, followed by the UK, France, and Nordic countries. Europe’s market is projected to triple from USD 6.76 billion in 2024 to USD 20.55 billion by 2035, driven by cloud expansion and green cooling technologies[41](https://menafn.com/1109772613/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4Energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv).

In APAC, Schneider is positioned in the fastest‑growing region, supported by rapid hyperscale and edge data center expansion in China, India, Japan, and Southeast Asia. APAC ranked second in global cooling investment in 2024, with China leading the region, followed by Australia, Japan, and India. The region’s CAGR is estimated at approximately 14.8 % to 16 %, reflecting strong demand for advanced cooling solutions[41](https://menafn.com/1109772613/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4Energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv).

### 9.2. Emerging markets and free‑cooling opportunities

Emerging markets across Latin America, Middle East & Africa (MEA), and parts of APAC present significant growth opportunities for Schneider Electric’s CDU offerings.

In Latin America, Brazil leads the data center cooling market, followed by Mexico, Chile, and Colombia. In MEA, South Africa dominates, with growing activity in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Israel, Nigeria, and Kenya. These regions are increasingly investing in digital infrastructure and modular cooling technologies[41](https://menafn.com/1109772613/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4Energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv).

Free‑cooling techniques—leveraging ambient air or water to reduce reliance on mechanical chillers—are gaining traction in regions with favorable climates. Western Europe, the Nordics, APAC, and North America are adopting free‑cooling systems, including water‑free chillers and optimized CRAC units. Schneider can capitalize on these trends by integrating free‑cooling capabilities into its CDU portfolio, especially in markets prioritizing sustainability and energy efficiency[18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html).

### 9.3. Market share and investment by region

Schneider Electric’s regional market share and investment dynamics reflect its strategic focus and competitive positioning.

A summary of regional market share and investment trends is presented below:

Title: Regional Market Share and Investment Dynamics

| Region           | Approximate Market Share (2024‑2025) | Investment Trends and Notes                                                                 |
|:----------------|:-------------------------------------:|:-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| North America   | ~53 % of global cooling investment    | U.S. dominates (~98 % of regional share); Schneider investing >USD 700 million through 2027; strong local manufacturing (83 % of goods sold locally) [41](https://menafn.com/1109772613/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4Energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv) |
| Europe          | ~20 % in Germany; growing overall     | Market projected to triple by 2035; strong regulatory push for green cooling; Schneider’s EcoStruxure and liquid cooling integration strengthen positioning [41](https://menafn.com/1109772613/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4Energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv) |
| Asia‑Pacific    | Second largest region; fast growth    | APAC CAGR ~14.8 %–16 %; China leads, followed by Australia, Japan, India; hyperscale expansion fuels demand [41](https://menafn.com/1109772613/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4Energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv) |
| Latin America & MEA | Emerging markets with rising activity | Brazil and South Africa lead; increasing adoption of modular and efficient cooling systems [41](https://menafn.com/1109772613/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4Energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv) |

Schneider’s regional investment strategy aligns with these dynamics, focusing on scaling manufacturing in North America, leveraging sustainability leadership in Europe, and expanding presence in high‑growth APAC and emerging markets.

### 9.4. Localization and regulatory adaptation strategies

Schneider Electric employs targeted localization and regulatory adaptation strategies to strengthen its regional competitiveness.

In North America, the company’s substantial U.S. investments—over USD 700 million through 2027—support localized manufacturing, supply chain resilience, and tariff mitigation. This enables faster delivery of CDU solutions and alignment with domestic regulatory and sustainability standards[30](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-invest-over-700-million-us-power-ai-boom-2025-03-25/).

In Europe, Schneider integrates its EcoStruxure platform with automated sustainability reporting to comply with the EU’s Energy Efficiency Directive. Its liquid cooling and AI‑optimized reference designs support compliance with stringent energy and carbon regulations, particularly in Germany, the Netherlands, and the Nordics[42](https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/ResearchInsight/green-data-center-gdc-market.asp).

In APAC and emerging markets, Schneider adapts to local conditions by offering modular, scalable CDU solutions suitable for hyperscale, edge, and colocation deployments. In regions with free‑cooling potential, Schneider can tailor systems to leverage ambient conditions, aligning with local energy efficiency incentives and infrastructure constraints. While specific localization programs in APAC and emerging markets are not detailed in available sources, the company’s global strategy suggests such adaptation is underway.

Overall, Schneider’s regional strategies combine investment, compliance, and product adaptation to reinforce its global leadership in data center cooling solutions.

## 10. Customer Case Studies

This section presents real‑world examples of Schneider Electric’s cooling solutions in action, focusing on high‑density AI/HPC deployments, performance metrics, customer satisfaction, and enterprise reference architectures. Each sub‑section provides factual, cited insights relevant to investor analysis.

### 10.1. High‑density AI/HPC deployments using Schneider cooling

One prominent example is the deployment of Motivair by Schneider Electric’s liquid cooling portfolio, which includes megawatt‑class Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs) designed for high‑density AI and HPC workloads. These CDUs support rack densities from 105 kW up to 2.5 MW and are certified for NVIDIA’s latest hardware. They are already deployed in six of the world’s top ten supercomputers, demonstrating their capability in extreme high‑density environments [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c).

### 10.2. Performance metrics: energy savings, PUE, ROI

University College Dublin (UCD) implemented Schneider Electric’s EcoStruxure Row Data Center solution with Uniflair InRow DX cooling. This upgrade reduced the data center’s PUE from 1.42 to 1.37, delivering measurable energy savings and supporting sustainability goals [43](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/schneider-electric-and-total-power-solutions-win-data-centre-consolidationupgrade-project-at-dcs-awards-2023-301838545.html).

Another case, DataXion—the largest data center in North Africa—deployed EcoStruxure solutions including In‑Row Cooling and achieved approximately 35.0% energy savings, 30.0% maintenance cost reduction, and a PUE below 1.6 [44](https://asiagrowthpartners.com/case-study/dataxion-enhancing-efficiency-and-reducing-energy-consumption-with-ecostruxure/c5115).

Title: Performance Metrics from Customer Deployments

| Deployment | PUE | Energy Savings (%) | Maintenance Cost Savings (%) |
|:---|---:|---:|---:|
| University College Dublin | 1.37 (from 1.42) | N/A | N/A |
| DataXion (North Africa) | <1.6 | 35.0 | 30.0 |

Source: Schneider Electric case studies [43](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/schneider-electric-and-total-power-solutions-win-data-centre-consolidationupgrade-project-at-dcs-awards-2023-301838545.html).

### 10.3. Customer satisfaction and operational outcomes

At University College Dublin, the upgraded cooling system not only improved energy efficiency but also freed up physical space by relocating cooling equipment to the roof, enabling redevelopment of new educational facilities—highlighting operational flexibility and customer satisfaction [43](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/schneider-electric-and-total-power-solutions-win-data-centre-consolidationupgrade-project-at-dcs-awards-2023-301838545.html).

DataXion reported enhanced workforce comfort by 20.0%, improved availability, and strengthened IT equipment functionality following Schneider’s solution deployment [44](https://asiagrowthpartners.com/case-study/dataxion-enhancing-efficiency-and-reducing-energy-consumption-with-ecostruxure/c5115).

### 10.4. Enterprise testimonials and reference architectures

In the UCD case, Schneider Electric and partner Total Power Solutions received the DCS Awards 2023 for the Data Centre Consolidation/Upgrade Project of the Year, underscoring the effectiveness of their reference architecture in a live, high‑density environment [43](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/schneider-electric-and-total-power-solutions-win-data-centre-consolidationupgrade-project-at-dcs-awards-2023-301838545.html).

While not specific to CDUs, EcoDataCenter in Sweden leveraged Schneider’s EcoStruxure platform—including Uniflair chillers and CRAH units—to achieve a PUE of 1.15 and 99% energy efficiency in UPS operation, with heat reuse contributing to sustainability. The CTO stated: “When we build a data center, we consider how we’re part of the community,” reflecting strong customer alignment with Schneider’s solutions [45](https://www.se.com/us/en/work/campaign/life-is-on/case-study/ecodatacenter/).

## 11. Risk Assessment

This section examines potential risks that Schneider Electric might face in the data center cooling market, including both external and internal challenges.

### 11.1. Competitive threats and technology disruption risks

Schneider Electric faces intensifying competition from established players such as Vertiv, ABB, Siemens, and immersion‑specialist firms, which may erode its market share in CDUs and liquid cooling solutions. According to industry commentary, competition from ABB, Siemens, and Vertiv is particularly intense in the U.S. market, posing a threat to Schneider’s pricing and positioning strategies [46](https://meyka.com/blog/schneider-electric-surge-data-centre-boom-with-confident-2025-outlook/). Additionally, emerging cooling technologies—such as two‑phase immersion and hybrid systems—could disrupt the market if they achieve lower total cost of ownership or faster deployment cycles. A 2025 analysis notes that while two‑phase immersion offers lower 10‑year TCO, its high upfront cost and integration complexity mean it will coexist with other technologies, but could still challenge Schneider’s offerings over time [47](https://www.facilitiesdive.com/news/2025-outlook-data-center-cooling-electricity-demand-ai-dual-phase-direct-to-chip-energy-efficiency/737920/).

### 11.2. Obsolescence and integration complexity

Rapid evolution in data center cooling—driven by AI workloads and increasing rack power densities—raises the risk of product obsolescence. Schneider’s CDUs, while scalable up to 2.5 MW, must continually adapt to future silicon and GPU architectures to remain relevant [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). Integration complexity is another concern: deploying liquid cooling systems requires end‑to‑end coordination across hardware, software, and services. As noted in Schneider’s own portfolio announcement, liquid cooling deployment is complex and demands rigorous testing and validation to avoid on‑site issues [8](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-unveils-liquid-cooling-portfolio-with-motivair-featuring-dedicated-solutions-and-services-for-hpc-and-ai-workloads-68da975376d417f4a10de42c). Failure to manage integration complexity could lead to deployment delays or customer dissatisfaction.

### 11.3. Supply chain and tariff vulnerabilities

Schneider Electric’s operations are exposed to supply chain disruptions and tariff volatility. Industry reports highlight that local supply chains are not fully equipped to support high‑density AI workloads, with 83 % of data center experts expressing concern over supply chain readiness for liquid‑cooled facilities [48](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/experts-believe-local-supply-chains-are-not-fully-equipped-to-support-high-density-ai-workloads/). Tariffs on critical materials such as steel and aluminum are increasing costs and delaying construction timelines, potentially impacting CDU production and delivery schedules [49](https://www.datacenterfrontier.com/hyperscale/article/55279670/how-tariffs-could-impact-data-centers-ai-and-energy-amid-supply-chain-shifts). Schneider has already acknowledged that U.S. tariffs could cost “a couple of hundred million” euros, and is offsetting this through pricing strategies and increased local manufacturing—83 % of its North American products are now made locally [7](https://www.reuters.com/business/schneider-electric-confirms-2025-outlook-data-centres-drive-growth-2025-07-31/). Nonetheless, continued tariff unpredictability and global supply chain fragility remain significant risks.

### 11.4. Regulatory and environmental compliance challenges

Schneider Electric must navigate evolving regulatory and environmental standards across global markets. Data centers are under scrutiny for their environmental and public health impacts: in the U.S., data center operations—including cooling and backup generators—have been linked to significant pollutant emissions, health costs, and regulatory fines [50](https://www.businessinsider.com/ai-runs-dirty-power-and-the-public-pays-the-price-2025-6). As regulators tighten emissions and water‑discharge standards, Schneider’s cooling solutions must comply with stricter environmental requirements. Additionally, cybersecurity risks in supply chains—such as counterfeit hardware or tampered components—pose compliance and safety challenges. Schneider addresses this through encrypted chips for traceability, but the threat remains a concern for data center infrastructure [51](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/01/17/how-colocation-providers-hyperscalers-defend-supply-chain-attacks/).

## 12. Investment Opportunity Analysis

This section provides a focused, investor‑oriented analysis of Schneider Electric’s cooling solutions, emphasizing strategic advantages and growth prospects in the AI‑driven data center cooling market.

### 12.1. Growth potential in AI‑driven cooling segment

The AI‑driven cooling segment is experiencing explosive growth, driven by surging demand for high‑density compute infrastructure. The global coolant distribution unit (CDU) market is projected to expand from USD 1.05 billion in 2025 to USD 7.74 billion by 2032, representing a robust compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 33.0 % [52](https://www.ainvest.com/news/rise-liquid-cooling-ai-driven-data-centers-impact-coolant-distribution-units-cdus-2511/). AI training workloads are expected to drive liquid cooling penetration to approximately 74 % by 2027 [52](https://www.ainvest.com/news/rise-liquid-cooling-ai-driven-data-centers-impact-coolant-distribution-units-cdus-2511/).

Schneider Electric is well positioned to capitalize on this trend. In Q1 2025, direct liquid cooling revenue more than doubled, and the total liquid cooling market is forecast to exceed USD 2.5 billion for full‑year 2025 [13](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-physical-infrastructure-grew-17-percent-yy-in-1q-2025-driven-by-ai-buildout-according-to-delloro-group-302497877.html). The company’s Motivair‑powered portfolio, unveiled in September 2025, includes CDUs capable of delivering 105 kW to 2.5 MW of cooling power—well ahead of current market demand [53](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).

These figures underscore a compelling growth trajectory for AI‑driven cooling, with Schneider Electric positioned to capture significant market share through advanced, scalable solutions.

### 12.2. Schneider’s competitive positioning and strategic advantages

Schneider Electric holds a near‑parity leadership position in the global data center physical infrastructure market, virtually tied with Vertiv in market share as of Q1 2025 [13](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-physical-infrastructure-grew-17-percent-yy-in-1q-2025-driven-by-ai-buildout-according-to-delloro-group-302497877.html). The acquisition of a 75 % stake in Motivair in early 2025 for USD 850 million significantly enhanced its liquid cooling capabilities, with plans to acquire the remaining stake by 2028 [5](https://www.reuters.com/markets/deals/schneider-electric-buy-data-centre-cooling-firm-motivair-850-million-2024-10-17/).

The Motivair‑powered portfolio offers a comprehensive, end‑to‑end liquid cooling ecosystem—including megawatt‑class CDUs, rear‑door heat exchangers, dynamic cold plates, chillers, and EcoStruxure software—backed by global supply chain and service infrastructure [53](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html). Schneider’s collaboration with NVIDIA on AI‑optimized reference designs enables up to 20 % reduction in cooling energy use and 30 % faster project development timelines [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7).

These strategic advantages—market leadership, comprehensive product portfolio, strong partnerships, and global execution capability—position Schneider Electric as a compelling investment in the cooling infrastructure space.

### 12.3. Investment thesis for cooling infrastructure leadership

Schneider Electric is uniquely positioned to lead the cooling infrastructure market, supported by several key factors:

- Accelerating AI‑driven demand: With AI workloads pushing rack densities toward 600 kW and beyond, liquid cooling is becoming essential. Schneider’s Motivair‑powered solutions are engineered for these high‑density environments [53](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Market leadership and scale: Schneider is virtually tied with Vertiv in global market share and benefits from a broad, integrated infrastructure portfolio spanning hardware, software, and services [13](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-physical-infrastructure-grew-17-percent-yy-in-1q-2025-driven-by-ai-buildout-according-to-delloro-group-302497877.html).
- Innovation and partnerships: Co‑development with NVIDIA and deployment in hyperscale environments (e.g., Equinix HK1) demonstrate Schneider’s ability to deliver cutting‑edge, energy‑efficient cooling solutions [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7).
- Financial strength and execution: The Motivair acquisition and rapid product rollout reflect Schneider’s capacity to invest and scale in high‑growth segments.

Together, these elements form a robust investment thesis: Schneider Electric is poised to capture outsized returns from the transition to AI‑optimized, liquid‑cooled data centers, supported by technological leadership, strategic partnerships, and global execution.

### 12.4. Key differentiators for future expansion

Schneider Electric’s unique differentiators that will drive future expansion include:

- Megawatt‑scale CDU capability: The Motivair CDU portfolio spans 105 kW to 2.5 MW, enabling support for next‑generation AI and HPC workloads [54](https://fmnewsroom.com/fm-news/powering-the-ai-future-schneider-electric-unveils-new-liquid-cooling-solutions/).
- Silicon‑level integration: Schneider is the only liquid cooling provider with proven expertise at the chip level, co‑developing solutions with NVIDIA and other GPU manufacturers [55](https://rss.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Comprehensive ecosystem: The portfolio includes CDUs, rear‑door heat exchangers, HDUs, dynamic cold plates, chillers, EcoStruxure software, and global services—delivering a full stack solution [55](https://rss.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Global manufacturing and service footprint: Motivair’s U.S. manufacturing is being expanded to Schneider’s facilities in Europe and Asia, ensuring scalable global delivery and support [56](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/03/19/cooling-ai-data-centers-schneider-electric-motivair-changing-game/).
- Proven deployment in hyperscale environments: The in‑rack CDU system deployed at Equinix HK1 delivers up to 150 kW per rack and achieves PUE of 1.2 or lower, demonstrating real‑world performance and energy savings [11](https://www.equinix.com/newsroom/press-releases/hongkong/2025/09/equinix-dell-technologies-and-schneider-electric-support-ai-infrastructure-in-hong-kong).

These differentiators—technical depth, ecosystem breadth, global scale, and proven performance—provide Schneider Electric with a durable competitive edge and a strong foundation for future market dominance.

## 13. ESG Considerations
