This section examines Schneider Electric’s environmental, social, and governance initiatives with a focus on sustainability practices in their cooling solutions, particularly relevant to investors evaluating long-term value and risk.

### 13.1. Energy‑efficiency and decarbonization in cooling solutions

Schneider Electric has made significant strides in enhancing energy efficiency and reducing carbon emissions in its cooling solutions. In December 2024, the company introduced a new AI‑optimized data center reference design co‑developed with NVIDIA, supporting liquid‑cooled, high‑density AI clusters up to 132 kW per rack. This design is projected to reduce cooling energy use by approximately 20 % and shorten project development time by around 30 %—both contributing to lower energy consumption and faster deployment of sustainable infrastructure [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7).

At Climate Week NYC 2025, Schneider Electric unveiled initiatives embedding lifecycle intelligence into its products via a partnership with Makersite. This enables automated creation of Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) data and Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs), facilitating decarbonization and eco‑design decision‑making [57](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/schneider-electric-puts-energy-resilience-at-the-center-of-climate-week-nyc-2025-302562515.html).

These efforts reflect a strategic approach to decarbonization—from chip‑to‑chiller—leveraging AI‑ready infrastructure, energy strategy consulting, and advanced hardware to reduce operational carbon footprints [58](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/12/04/2991515/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Announces-New-Solutions-to-Address-the-Energy-and-Sustainability-Challenges-Spurred-by-AI.html).

### 13.2. Sustainability metrics and water‑usage reduction

Schneider Electric has enhanced its sustainability reporting capabilities through its EcoStruxure IT DCIM software, which now includes automated sustainability reporting features. These enable data center operators to track metrics such as PUE, power consumption, and water usage, supporting compliance with the European Energy Efficiency Directive [33](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/schneider-electric-adds-automated-sustainability-reporting-to-ecostruxure-it-dcim-software/).

While specific water‑usage reduction figures for Schneider’s cooling solutions are not publicly disclosed, industry context underscores the importance of this metric. According to the International Energy Agency, a 100 MW data center can consume up to 2 000 000 liters of water per day for cooling—highlighting the critical need for water‑efficient technologies [59](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_center).

Schneider’s tools for sustainability reporting and lifecycle assessment position it to help clients reduce both energy and water consumption, though more granular data on water‑usage improvements in its CDU portfolio would strengthen investor insight.

### 13.3. Environmental impact of liquid vs. air cooling

Schneider Electric’s research and deployments underscore the environmental advantages of liquid cooling over traditional air cooling. A white paper (#282) comparing immersive liquid‑cooled versus air‑cooled large data centers found that at higher rack densities (20 kW/rack and above), liquid cooling delivers CapEx savings of 10 % at 20 kW/rack and 14 % at 40 kW/rack, while also reducing energy and water consumption [60](https://www.idomaining.org/datacenter/schneider-electric-white-paper-liquid-cooled-vs-air-cooled-large-data-centers/).

In a real‑world deployment, Schneider’s in‑rack direct‑to‑chip liquid cooling CDU system at Equinix’s HK1 data center in Hong Kong achieved a PUE of 1.2 or lower, compared to 1.4–1.5 for traditional CRAC systems. The system also offers potential energy savings exceeding 2 000 MWh annually per data center if fully implemented [11](https://www.equinix.com/newsroom/press-releases/hongkong/2025/09/equinix-dell-technologies-and-schneider-electric-support-ai-infrastructure-in-hong-kong).

These findings demonstrate that liquid cooling not only improves thermal performance but also significantly reduces environmental impact through lower energy and water usage, aligning with sustainability goals.

### 13.4. Alignment with global data center sustainability goals

Schneider Electric’s strategies align closely with global sustainability objectives in the data center industry. Its liquid cooling solutions support high‑density AI workloads while delivering improved energy efficiency and reduced environmental impact, addressing the growing demand for sustainable infrastructure [53](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).

The company’s participation in Climate Week NYC 2025, with initiatives focused on energy resilience, smart water management, and lifecycle intelligence, underscores its commitment to global climate action and sustainable infrastructure development [57](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/schneider-electric-puts-energy-resilience-at-the-center-of-climate-week-nyc-2025-302562515.html).

Recognition by TIME as the most sustainable company in June 2025 further validates Schneider’s leadership in sustainability. Its liquid cooling portfolio, enhanced by the Motivair acquisition, completes its chip‑to‑chiller offering and reinforces its role in enabling sustainable AI infrastructure [61](https://sustainabilitymag.com/company-reports/headline-maximum-55-60-characters-how-schneider-electri).

Overall, Schneider Electric’s ESG initiatives in cooling solutions are well aligned with industry sustainability mandates, positioning the company as a credible partner for investors seeking environmentally responsible growth.

## 14. Future Outlook (2025 and beyond)

This section explores the anticipated trajectory of data center cooling technologies and markets beyond 2025, with a focus on Schneider Electric’s strategic positioning and innovation roadmap. It builds on prior analysis of Schneider’s recent gains in liquid cooling, AI-optimized infrastructure, and global expansion, projecting how these foundations will support future growth and leadership in the evolving cooling landscape.

### 14.1. Market forecasts and emerging cooling technologies

The global data center cooling market is projected to grow significantly beyond 2025. Recent forecasts estimate the market will expand from approximately USD 19.8 billion in 2024 to USD 50.9 billion by 2030, reflecting a CAGR of 17.0 % [15](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/11/05/3181176/0/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Industry-Business-Research-Report-2025-2030-Cloud-Expansion-IoT-Growth-Energy-Efficient-Tech-Adoption-and-Uptime-Demands-Drive-Investments.html). Other projections suggest a rise from USD 16.3 billion in 2024 to USD 40.7 billion by 2030 (CAGR 16.5 %) [18](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/07/08/3111518/28124/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-Report-2025-2030-Featuring-4energy-3M-Asetek-Black-Box-Carrier-Condair-Daikin-Delta-Johnson-Controls-Mitsubishi-STULZ-Schneider-Rittal-Vertiv.html). A third source estimates growth from USD 19.6 billion in 2024 to USD 32.9 billion by 2030 (CAGR 13.1 %) [62](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/03/05/3037662/0/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Outlook-2030-Cooling-Technologies-Evolve-for-AI-HPC-Workloads-Market-Report-Future-Trends.html).

Emerging cooling technologies—particularly liquid cooling, immersion, AI-driven smart management, and modular designs—are key growth drivers. Liquid-based systems already account for nearly 46 % of the cooling market and are expected to grow at a 17.5 % CAGR through 2031 [63](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/global-data-center-cooling-market-industry). Smart cooling systems leveraging IoT sensors and AI for real-time optimization are gaining traction [15](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/11/05/3181176/0/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Industry-Business-Research-Report-2025-2030-Cloud-Expansion-IoT-Growth-Energy-Efficient-Tech-Adoption-and-Uptime-Demands-Drive-Investments.html).

Schneider Electric is well-positioned to capitalize on these trends. Its acquisition of Motivair strengthens its liquid cooling portfolio, while its AI-ready reference designs and EcoStruxure platform align with the shift toward intelligent, sustainable cooling solutions [21](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/data-center-cooling-market-to-grow-at-a-cagr-of-16-21-from-2025-to-2032--skyquest-technology-consulting-302520514.html).

### 14.2. Evolution of AI‑optimized and immersion cooling

AI workloads are driving unprecedented energy and thermal demands. The International Energy Agency projects that electricity demand from AI data centers will more than double by 2030, with AI-specific centers alone expected to quadruple consumption [64](https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2025/apr/10/energy-demands-from-ai-datacentres-to-quadruple-by-2030-says-report).

In response, AI-optimized cooling—combining liquid cooling with intelligent control—is emerging as a critical innovation. Schneider’s collaboration with NVIDIA has produced reference architectures supporting up to 132 kW per rack, reducing cooling energy use by 20 % and accelerating deployment timelines by 30 % [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7).

Immersion cooling is also gaining momentum. Industry players are investing heavily in immersion technologies, and the superior thermal performance of liquid immersion is increasingly recognized as essential for high-density AI and HPC environments [65](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250715819114/en/Data-Center-Cooling-Market-Landscape-2025-2030-Surge-in-Adoption-of-AI-Will-Drive-Demand-for-Advanced-Technologies-Rising-Rack-Power-Densities-Transform-Data-Center-Designs-and-Cooling-Needs---ResearchAndMarkets.com).

Looking ahead, Schneider Electric is likely to deepen its AI-optimized cooling capabilities by integrating advanced control systems—potentially leveraging reinforcement learning and digital twins—to optimize CDU operations dynamically. Academic developments such as LC‑Opt demonstrate the potential for RL-based control of liquid cooling systems [66](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.00116).

### 14.3. Expansion vectors: edge, hyperscale, retrofit markets

Three key growth vectors will shape Schneider’s future expansion:

1. Edge computing: Smaller, distributed edge sites require compact, efficient cooling. Liquid modules and factory-sealed CDUs are ideal for unmanned, high-density edge deployments [63](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/global-data-center-cooling-market-industry).

2. Hyperscale data centers: Hyperscalers continue to drive demand for high-density cooling. In the U.S., the data center cooling market is expected to grow from USD 5.96 billion in 2025 to USD 17.32 billion by 2030 (CAGR 23.8 %) [67](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/united-states-data-center-cooling-market). Projects like IREN’s 75 MW AI data center in Texas, designed for 200 kW per rack via direct-to-chip cooling, exemplify this trend [68](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/us-hyperscale-data-center-market-092300001.html).

3. Retrofit markets: Regulatory incentives and sustainability mandates are accelerating retrofits. In the U.S., federal and state incentives are shortening retrofit payback periods, while cooling now accounts for up to 35 % of data center capex [67](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/united-states-data-center-cooling-market).

Schneider Electric’s modular CDU offerings, AI-optimized designs, and strong service ecosystem position it to capture growth across these vectors.

### 14.4. Long‑term strategic direction for Schneider Electric

Schneider Electric’s long-term strategy beyond 2025 is likely to focus on four pillars:

- Deepening AI‑ready infrastructure: Building on its NVIDIA partnership and EcoStruxure platform, Schneider will likely expand its portfolio of AI-optimized reference designs, integrating CDUs, immersion, and intelligent control systems [32](https://www.businessinsider.com/schneider-electric-nvidia-ai-data-centers-designs-energy-demands-2025-7).

- Advancing liquid and immersion cooling: With Motivair integrated, Schneider is poised to lead in immersion and direct-to-chip cooling, targeting hyperscale and HPC markets.

- Expanding modular and edge solutions: Schneider will likely develop compact, plug-and-play CDU modules tailored for edge and retrofit applications, leveraging its manufacturing scale and global service network.

- Embedding sustainability and AI-driven energy intelligence: Schneider’s “bend the energy curve” vision emphasizes using AI to optimize energy consumption across data centers and broader value chains [10](https://www.se.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/news/press-releases/schneider-electric-announces-new-solutions-to-address-the-energy-and-sustainability-challenges-spurred-by-ai-674f1aaeda55de201f0ca8c4).

In summary, Schneider Electric is positioned to lead the next wave of data center cooling innovation—anchored in AI-optimized liquid technologies, modular deployment, and sustainability—driving growth across hyperscale, edge, and retrofit markets well beyond 2025.

## 15. Appendix: Technical Specifications

This section provides detailed technical insights into Schneider Electric’s coolant distribution units (CDUs) and related cooling products, offering investors a deeper understanding of product capabilities, performance, integration, and advanced control features.

### 15.1. Technical comparison of cooling product lines

Schneider Electric’s liquid cooling portfolio, under the Motivair by Schneider Electric brand, spans multiple CDU variants tailored for different deployment scenarios:

- Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs): Scalable from 105 kW to 2.5 MW, designed in collaboration with leading silicon manufacturers and certified for NVIDIA hardware, enabling megawatt‑scale cooling for AI and HPC environments [40](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- ChilledDoor rear‑door heat exchangers: Rack‑agnostic units capable of cooling up to 75 kW per rack, ideal for GPU‑intensive workloads [40](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Liquid‑to‑Air Heat Dissipation Units (HDU™): Compact units (600 mm width) delivering 100 kW of heat rejection, or up to 132 kW when creating a water loop, matching NVIDIA NVL144 architecture [40](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Floor‑standing (liquid‑to‑liquid) CDUs: Isolate facility water from the IT loop, optimized for high‑density AI workloads [69](https://www.se.com/us/en/product-subcategory/368881735-coolant-distribution-units/).

Advantages:

- Scalability: From rack‑level (ChilledDoor, HDU) to room‑level (CDUs up to 2.5 MW).
- Flexibility: Rack‑agnostic designs and compact footprints support diverse deployment models.
- Efficiency: Engineered for high‑density AI workloads with optimized thermal control.
- Integration: Designed for seamless compatibility with next‑gen processors and accelerators.

### 15.2. Performance benchmarks and efficiency metrics

While Schneider Electric does not publicly disclose detailed numeric benchmarks for each product, available data and market context provide insight into performance:

- CDU portfolio supports megawatt‑scale cooling (105 kW to 2.5 MW), enabling deployment in top supercomputers [40](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Liquid cooling solutions can reduce cooling‑related energy consumption by up to 40% compared to traditional air cooling [70](https://www.intelmarketresearch.com/distributed-coolant-distribution-units-2025-2032-497-4596).
- Adaptive cooling controls are present in approximately 65% of new CDU installations in Europe and North America as of 2022, reflecting efficiency‑driven adoption [71](https://pmarketresearch.com/auto/high-cooling-capacity-coolant-distribution-units-market/).
- Schneider’s In‑Row Direct Expansion CDUs (as referenced in market analysis) achieve approximately 20% better energy efficiency than fixed‑speed alternatives [71](https://pmarketresearch.com/auto/high-cooling-capacity-coolant-distribution-units-market/).

Title: Performance and Efficiency Highlights

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|:---|---:|:---|
| Cooling capacity range | 105 kW – 2 500 kW | CDU portfolio scale [40](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html) |
| Energy savings vs air cooling | up to 40.0% | Liquid cooling efficiency [70](https://www.intelmarketresearch.com/distributed-coolant-distribution-units-2025-2032-497-4596) |
| Efficiency gain vs fixed‑speed CDUs | 20.0% | In‑Row Direct Expansion units [71](https://pmarketresearch.com/auto/high-cooling-capacity-coolant-distribution-units-market/) |

These figures underscore the high performance and energy efficiency of Schneider Electric’s cooling solutions.

### 15.3. Integration capabilities with data center infrastructure

Schneider Electric’s cooling products are engineered for seamless integration into modern data center environments:

- Designed in collaboration with silicon manufacturers and certified for NVIDIA hardware, ensuring compatibility with next‑gen processors and accelerators [40](https://www.globenewswire.com/de/news-release/2025/09/29/3157885/0/en/Schneider-Electric-Unveils-Liquid-Cooling-Portfolio-with-Motivair-Featuring-Dedicated-Solutions-and-Services-for-HPC-and-AI-Workloads.html).
- Fully integrated with EcoStruxure modular and pod architectures, including NetShelter racks, enabling streamlined deployment in modular AI data centers [72](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/07/09/tackling-complexity-in-ai-data-centers-leveraging-fully-integrated-solutions/).
- EcoStruxure platform supports connectivity via IoT, sensors, cloud, and analytics, enabling unified management of power, cooling, and infrastructure [73](https://www.se.com/us/en/work/solutions/for-business/data-centers-and-networks/).
- Embedded sensors and connectivity from design phase support condition‑based maintenance and avoid costly retrofits [74](https://www.se.com/ww/en/insights/ai-and-technology/artificial-intelligence/transforming-data-center-services-ai-driven-condition-based-maintenance/).

These integration capabilities reduce deployment complexity, accelerate time‑to‑market, and enhance operational resilience.

### 15.4. AI‑enabled control and monitoring features

Schneider Electric embeds advanced AI‑driven control and monitoring across its cooling solutions:

- AI‑driven condition‑based maintenance (CBM) integrates diverse data streams, digital twins, and analytics to reduce on‑site maintenance visits by up to 40% and lower total cost of ownership by 20% [74](https://www.se.com/ww/en/insights/ai-and-technology/artificial-intelligence/transforming-data-center-services-ai-driven-condition-based-maintenance/).
- EcoStruxure software leverages AI to optimize air and water flow, temperature, and energy use in real time, especially in environments combining air and liquid cooling [75](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/08/18/the-critical-role-of-software-in-modern-data-center-operations-and-ai-infrastructure/).
- Predictive maintenance capabilities include filter replacement, coil cleaning, and water quality assessments, shifting from calendar‑based to condition‑based schedules [75](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/08/18/the-critical-role-of-software-in-modern-data-center-operations-and-ai-infrastructure/).
- Digital twin models simulate thermal and electrical infrastructure, enabling optimized design, failure simulation, and energy consumption analysis [75](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/08/18/the-critical-role-of-software-in-modern-data-center-operations-and-ai-infrastructure/).

These AI‑enabled features enhance reliability, reduce operational costs, and support proactive infrastructure management.

## 16. References and Citations

This section compiles all sources referenced throughout the report, ensuring proper accreditation and transparency for investor review.

### 16.1. References format

All references in this report should follow a consistent citation style to maintain professionalism and clarity. Use the following guidelines:

- Format: Author or Organization. Year. Title. Source or Publisher. URL (if applicable). Accessed Date.
- For financial data: include the company name, fiscal year or quarter, report title (e.g., Annual Report 2024), page or table number, and URL if available.
- For market data and forecasts: cite the research firm or publication, report title, publication date, and page or section.
- For news or press releases: include the issuing organization, date of release, title, and URL.
- For case studies or technical specifications: cite the customer or project name, Schneider Electric documentation or press release, date, and URL.
- In-text citations should correspond to full references listed here, using a numbered or author-date system consistently.

Example entries:
Schneider Electric SE. 2025. Annual Report 2024. Schneider Electric. Accessed November 19, 2025.
Motivair by Schneider Electric. 2024. Product Brochure: Megawatt‑Scale CDUs. Schneider Electric. Accessed November 19, 2025.
Gartner. 2025. Data Center Cooling Market Forecast 2025–2032. Gartner Research. Accessed November 19, 2025.
Vertiv. 2023. Vertiv In‑Row Cooling Solutions Overview. Vertiv. Accessed November 19, 2025.

Ensure each reference includes sufficient detail for verification and aligns with the report’s professional and investor‑focused tone.
